summarization.a: The image illustrates a sequence of steps involving nitrogen (N₂), vacuum, hydrofluoric acid (HF), and trimethylamine (TMA) treatments on a moving substrate.

a.question: How many gas slits are required for a single ALD cycle and what is the main used gas?
a.answer: a	There is a minimum of five gas slits, most of them are used for the carrier and purge gas nitrogen.

a.question: Is the full substrate exposed to a single gas at a time?
a.answer: a	No, the substrate is likely way bigger than the distance between slits, meaning that not the entire substrate is exposed to a single gas at a time.

a.question: Is there an actual vacuum between the gas slits or is it used to depict a flow towards a pump?
a.answer: a	There is no actual vacuum as there is a constant flow of gas there.

a.question: Why is HF dosed first?
a.answer: a	If TMA is dosed first, there has not yet been modification so there will be no etching.
